mnemonics.title: Degree Ladder
mnemonics.content: 1 = Line, 2 = Parabola (Quadratic), 3 = S-curve (Cubic). Each step up adds more flexibility and complexity.

mnemonics.title: Linear in Parameters
mnemonics.content: Polynomial regression is non-linear in FEATURES but LINEAR in PARAMETERS. It is solved using OLS just like linear regression on transformed features.

mnemonics.title: Goldilocks Degree
mnemonics.content: Too low = underfits (straight line for curved data). Too high = overfits (wiggly curve through noise). Just right = cross-validation to find optimal degree.

mnemonics.title: Transform then Regress
mnemonics.content: Step 1: Create X², X³... features. Step 2: Run linear regression. That is polynomial regression - feature transformation + OLS.

mnemonics.title: Scale Before Square
mnemonics.content: Always scale features BEFORE creating polynomial terms. X=1000 means X²=1,000,000 - huge scale differences wreck optimization.
